_comment: Composite indexes deployed via `gcloud firestore indexes composite create` or `gcloud firestore deploy --indexes-file`. IMPL-04 adds the metric+sampleTime index for body composition range queries. IMPL-12 adds the goals composite index and steps collection-group field overrides. The single-field index on users.googleHealth.healthUserId is created automatically by Firestore — no entry needed here.

indexes._comment: IMPL-20 #6: BloodReadingRepository.findLatestByMarker — whereEqualTo(marker) + orderBy(sampleDate DESC).limit(1). The bodyComposition entry above already covers BodyCompositionRepository.findLatest (metric ASC, sampleTime DESC). DailyMetricRepository.findLatestDailyMetric only needs the auto-created single-field `date` index, so no composite entry is required for it.

fieldOverrides._comment: IMPL-AND-20 #10: the unified delta-read now reads each subcollection by per-user enumeration — it lists the user's own parent docs (medications, goals, goalChatThreads, nutritionDays, goals/phases) and runs a COLLECTION-scoped `<leaf>.orderBy(updatedAt ASC) [+ updatedAt >= cursor range]` query on each child collection directly. That is served by the automatic COLLECTION-scope single-field `updatedAt` index, so the previous COLLECTION_GROUP `updatedAt` overrides on adherence/history/phases/steps/messages/entries are no longer required and were removed (no collectionGroup(updatedAt) query remains anywhere). Top-level per-user collections (bloodReadings, medications, goals, locations, etc.) likewise need NO composite/override: their delta query is the same pure single-field updatedAt order+range the automatic COLLECTION-scope index already serves (tombstones are emitted, so no extra equality filter on syncStatus is combined in the delta path). The remaining overrides below back unrelated collectionGroup queries (steps metric/kind, medications.drugId) and array-contains (locations.equipmentIds).
